input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A static title card appears on a black background. Orange text reads Halloween Jack o' Lantern on the top line. The year 2010 sits centered below in the same color. No movement occurs as the text remains fixed. No changes occur now.</think><action>silent</action>

Rules: retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR text, numbers, and state changes.

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A static title card appears on a black background with orange text reading "Halloween Jack o' Lantern" on the top line and "2010" centered below. No movement occurs. [4-8] The scene transitions to a kitchen interior showing a large, ribbed orange pumpkin with a curved green stem sitting on a beige tiled island. Light wood cabinets, a doorway, and a stainless steel refrigerator are visible in the background. [8-12] A red-handled carving tool is inserted vertically into the pumpkin's top. A bright orange plastic bowl appears on the right counter, and small red carving tools lie scattered on the left surface. The inserted tool leans slightly leftward as the pumpkin remains stationary on the island.

From 14s to 18s, a red-handled carving tool is inserted into the upper left side of a large orange pumpkin, shifting from a diagonal angle to point horizontally left. The pumpkin's removed circular lid with a dark green stem rests on the beige tiled counter to the right, next to a bright orange bowl. Between 18s and 20s, the kitchen lighting dims significantly, darkening the background cabinets, while the tool is repositioned near the upper rim. By 22s, the tool points vertically upward from the top ridge, revealing a carved opening on the pumpkin's upper left surface. From 22s to 26s, the tool moves to the right side and is then inserted vertically into the upper surface to create a downward cut line, while the previously carved hole remains visible. Throughout the sequence, the detached lid and orange bowl remain stationary on the counter.

[12-14] The pumpkin's top lid is removed, exposing stringy yellow interior pulp, and the circular lid with its green stem rests on the tiled counter to the right; the red carving tool is no longer visible. [26-30] A second carved opening appears on the upper right side, mirroring the left eye, as the red-handled carving tool repositions diagonally in the center then inserts into the lower-left area. [30-32] The tool is removed, revealing a new triangular nose carved into the center. [32-38] The red tool inserts into the lower right side, then moves horizontally to the lower left and far left to carve the mouth's curve, while the detached lid and orange bowl remain stationary on the counter.

From [38-46], a red-handled carving tool repositions across the lower pumpkin face to carve a jagged mouth below the triangular nose and eyes. After the tool is removed, the interior illuminates with a yellow glow. At [46-48], the detached lid featuring a curved green stem is placed back atop the pumpkin, sitting slightly askew next to a stationary orange bowl. From [48-50], the kitchen lights extinguish, darkening the cabinets and counters while the carved face glows with intense orange light. Finally, from [50-52], the camera focuses closely on the illuminated jack-o'-lantern face against a pitch-black background, cropping out the stem and surrounding kitchen elements.

recent_thinks: [52-54] The camera holds a steady close-up on the illuminated pumpkin face against a pitch-black background. The internal orange light flickers slightly, highlighting the triangular eyes, nose, and jagged mouth with a single central tooth. The carved edges glow brightly while no surrounding kitchen elements remain visible in the total darkness surrounding the pumpkin.

[54-56] The camera slowly zooms in on the glowing jack-o'-lantern face set against a pitch-black background. The internal orange light illuminates the triangular eyes, nose, and jagged mouth with a central tooth. The light flickers slightly, highlighting the rough carved edges while the surrounding kitchen environment remains completely dark and unseen.

[56-58] The close-up view of the glowing pumpkin face cuts to a black screen. Centered orange text in a serif font reads "Happy Halloween!!!" with three exclamation marks. The previous scene of the illuminated carving and dark kitchen is completely replaced by this final title card.

[58-60] The static title card persists on a pitch-black background featuring centered orange serif text displaying "Happy Halloween!!!" with three exclamation marks. No visual alterations occur during this final interval as the bright orange lettering remains fixed in position without any movement or fading against the completely dark void.

[60-62] The screen continues displaying the static title card on a pitch black background with centered orange serif text reading Happy Halloween and three exclamation points. No visual changes occur during this interval as the bright orange lettering remains fixed in position without movement or fading against the dark void surrounding the text.

[62-64] The centered text reading Happy Halloween with three exclamation marks remains stationary on the black background. The orange color of the serif font dims significantly, shifting to a darker orange hue compared to the previous bright lettering. The text remains legible despite the reduction in brightness against the dark void surrounding the letters.

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A static title card appears on a black background with orange text reading "Halloween Jack o' Lantern" on the top line and "2010" centered below. No movement occurs. [4-8] The scene transitions to a kitchen interior showing a large, ribbed orange pumpkin with a curved green stem sitting on a beige tiled island. Light wood cabinets, a doorway, and a stainless steel refrigerator are visible in the background. [8-12] A red-handled carving tool is inserted vertically into the pumpkin's top. A bright orange plastic bowl appears on the right counter, and small red carving tools lie scattered on the left surface. The inserted tool leans slightly leftward as the pumpkin remains stationary on the island.

From 14s to 18s, a red-handled carving tool is inserted into the upper left side of a large orange pumpkin, shifting from a diagonal angle to point horizontally left. The pumpkin's removed circular lid with a dark green stem rests on the beige tiled counter to the right, next to a bright orange bowl. Between 18s and 20s, the kitchen lighting dims significantly, darkening the background cabinets, while the tool is repositioned near the upper rim. By 22s, the tool points vertically upward from the top ridge, revealing a carved opening on the pumpkin's upper left surface. From 22s to 26s, the tool moves to the right side and is then inserted vertically into the upper surface to create a downward cut line, while the previously carved hole remains visible. Throughout the sequence, the detached lid and orange bowl remain stationary on the counter.

The red-handled carving tool is repositioned diagonally in the center area between the two openings. The detached lid with the green stem and the orange bowl remain stationary on the tiled counter.

[28-30] The red-handled carving tool repositions from the center to the lower-left area of the pumpkin, inserting into the surface at a downward angle to carve a new feature. input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
